world.environment: steam-powered factory / airship dock / guild hall
world.cultural_context: A world where steam technology advanced faster and further. Mechanical automatons assist workers. Aetheric telegraphs connect cities. The Inventors' Guild controls the patents that power civilization. A Luddite resistance movement sabotages Guild infrastructure. The crime is sabotage of a steam core that killed a senior Guild engineer — but was it really an accident, a political attack, or targeted murder disguised as sabotage?
physics_constraints: No digital technology — mechanical computation only
Evidence can be destroyed by steam blast or fire
Automaton witnesses exist but their testimony is not legally admissible

expected_factions: Luddite Resistance (saboteurs, politically opposed to mechanization)
Factory Workers (caught between — some sympathize with resistance, some loyal to Guild)
character_archetype_hints: Guild patent holder (suspect — may have faked sabotage to eliminate a rival)
Luddite organizer (suspect — politically motivated, but did they actually do this specific act?)
factory floor foreman (witness — knows the technical details)
automaton maintenance engineer (suspect — had access to the steam core)
Guild inspector (investigator or suspect — has authority but possible conflict of interest)
key_clue_type_hints: physical: deliberate mechanical failure vs. accidental — tool marks, worn components that shouldn't be worn
chemical: accelerant residue indicating the explosion was set, not accidental
documentary: patent documents or financial records showing who benefits from the victim's death
environmental: timing of the explosion relative to shifts — who had access when
testimonial: a factory worker who saw someone near the steam core at the wrong time — but fears Guild retaliation for speaking
interrogation_mechanic_notes: Guild hierarchy is rigid — workers won't easily contradict Guild officials. The Luddite organizer is a suspect but may also be an ally in uncovering a Guild conspiracy. Automaton witnesses are available but legally non-admissible — they can still provide leads.

validator_expectations.note: The Luddite connection is the obvious red herring. The actual motive should be more personal — rivalry within the Guild.

generation_notes: The steampunk technology should be evidence-active, not just decorative. Mechanical failure analysis, steam pressure logs, aetheric telegraph records — these are the tech-appropriate equivalents of forensic investigation. The alternate history should feel like 'plausible 1890s with more steam' not 'Victorian with magic.'
